notes: Roman Christian nobleman known for his devotion and correspondence with Church Fathers such as Augustine and Jerome; recognized as a saint.

docs.title: Letter CXXIV
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: 1. About ten years ago that saintly man Pammachius sent me a copy of a certain person's rendering, or rather misrendering, of Origen's First Principles; with a request that in a Latin version I should give the true sense of the Greek and should set down the writer's words for good

docs.title: Letter LXVI
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … among them all my Pammachius is the wisest, the mightiest, and the noblest; great among the great, a leader among leaders, he is the commander in chief of all monks.

docs.title: Letter LXXVII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: I blushed to say more to one so learned or to give him back his own thoughts: lest I should seem less the consoler of a friend than the officious instructor of one already perfect.
